title: Twitter @gm8xx8
summary:
GTE-ModernColBERT (PyLate): 128D MaxSim retriever fine-tuned on ms-marco-en-bge-gemma

- Built on gte-modernbert-base
- Native to PyLate: supports reranking + HNSW indexing
- 32-token queries | 300-token docs
- Late interaction w/ dense 128D vectors

NanoBEIR:
- Acc@10: 0.91 | https://t.co/3rDrgYm4wl

title: Twitter @antoine_chaffin
summary:
Besides versatility, this also highlighted the potential of PyLate
The ModernBERT-base checkpoint achieves 51.3 of BEIR average
This means that we beat e5 in a <45 minutes training on MS MARCO only (using only half of the memory of our 8x100)
https://t.co/GDF7C5YN06 https://t.co/0ekEAkNQmN

title: Twitter @antoine_chaffin
summary:
Starting with my beloved PyLate
We have a lot of experiments on ColBERT models in the paper, with tons of different base models
PyLate handled it all, even models using half-baked remote code
This was a really cool stress test and I am really happy it went so smoothly https://t.co/jdJ2kmFIQW

title: Twitter @ihor_step
summary: The issue can be related to positional encoding mechanisms used by ModernBERT and DeBERTa. Currently, I am working with GLiNER based on ModernBERT and it achieved better performance than DeBERTa for bi-encoder architecture but not for uni-encoder.

title: Twitter @antoine_chaffin
summary:
This is impressive because most encoders trade capabilities on some tasks to be better on other
ModernBERT, on the other hand, is the first model to beat DeBERTa on GLUE, and does it while beating RoBERTa and GTE on BEIR
It is also in a league of its own for code, thanks to its
